description: Research spike: How should the reference library represent and weight ecosystem-level architectures vs single-repo applications? Must resolve before EPIC-010 implementation. Three investigation threads: taxonomy definition, weighting simulation, presentation format. Gate: taxonomy well-defined, double-counting quantified, frequency format validated.
title: Ecosystem Statistical Modeling Research

close_reason: Complete: 163 entries classified on 2-axis taxonomy. 43 removals identified (36 library/framework, 7 non-software). 65 production/platform, 19 production/application, 36 reference/application. Full results in taxonomy-classification.md.
description: Classify all 163 Discovered catalog entries on two axes: scope (platform/ecosystem | application) and use-type (production-grade | reference). Identify Library/Framework entries for removal from catalog. Output: complete classification with per-entry rationale + removal list.
title: Classify 163 catalog entries on 2-axis taxonomy

close_reason: Complete: Star-weighting has negligible impact (no style moves >2 ranks). Equal weighting recommended. Key finding: removing reference implementations is what changes rankings — DDD/Hexagonal/CQRS/Microservices drop significantly (tutorial bias removed), Modular Monolith and Plugin/Microkernel rise.
description: Design a weighting function based on GitHub stars for production-grade entries. Test candidates: linear stars, log(stars), star tiers, normalized percentiles. Compare unweighted vs star-weighted frequency tables. Reference entries carry zero weight. Key metrics: rank position changes >2, percentage changes >5 points, consistency with KataLog/AOSA.
title: Design and test star-weighted production ranking

close_reason: Abandoned: Presentation format deferred — belongs to the epic doing the reference library rewrite, not to this spike.
description: Draft 2-3 candidate formats for how ecosystem data appears in reference library documents. Evaluate each for clarity (can reader understand what's counted?), comparability (can you compare with KataLog?), and actionability (does it help architects decide?). Formats to consider: separate Ecosystem Frequency table, combined table with source column, flagged entries in existing tables.
title: Draft presentation formats for ecosystem data in reference library

close_reason: Complete: All 3 gates pass. Taxonomy 97% unambiguous, equal weighting recommended (star-weighting has no impact), 43 removals identified. Spike outcome: GO for EPIC-010.
description: Assess revised gate criteria: (1) Taxonomy validated — 2-axis classification produces unambiguous assignment for >=95% of entries. (2) Star-weighting model defined and tested — improves consistency with cross-source evidence. (3) Library/Framework removal list complete.
